danger_signals.visual_form: Alien sprite designs in sector environments
danger_signals.meaning: Combat threat; areas heavily guarded by hostile forces
Instant death threat; unique to Power Blade (player dies if falls off screen)

danger_signals.visual_form: Security guard/alien overlord sprite in designated security rooms
danger_signals.meaning: High-threat confrontation; barrier to sector progression
Resource scarcity; failure state if time runs out

danger_signals.visual_form: Life meter visual decrease on HUD
danger_signals.meaning: Damage accumulation; loss condition when meter reaches zero

safety_signals.meaning: Safe interaction; provides ID card access to restricted areas

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible items (boomerang power, range, quantity enhancements; Power Suit helmet)
safety_signals.meaning: Player empowerment; capability increase reduces danger

safety_signals.visual_form: Sector tape units obtained after boss defeat
safety_signals.meaning: Progress marker; completion of sector objectives
Progress preservation; ability to resume at specific checkpoints mitigates failure

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Master Computer control center; data tapes as authority tokens
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Six sectors arranged around central control center; database security rooms; Control Center as final hub

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Alien sprite designs; final boss (alien overlord/David Uncle transformed)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple alien enemy types per sector; regional gameplay differences suggest enemy variety
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Heavy guard presence throughout all six sectors; hostile occupation of databases

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Power Blade (energy boomerang weapon); Power Suit armor; NOVA protagonist sprite (buff soldier aesthetic)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player equipment progression; visual transformation when Power Suit equipped

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Master Computer operational; sectors functioning normally with minimal visible damage
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs present in sectors; routine database operations; agents available for contact

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sector environments occupied by alien forces; security systems activated; hostile presence throughout
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies patrol sectors; security guards defend database rooms; agents in hiding requiring discovery

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sector-specific visual change after tape retrieval; new cutscene with 'Sector Disarmed' music (BGM0A)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Reduced or eliminated enemy presence; sector marked as cleared; progress toward Control Center access

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Map screen shows all sectors cleared; access to Master Computer Control Center unlocked
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Final boss encounter available; alien overlord transformation sequence triggers

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Control center secured; epilogue cutscene plays (BGM10 in US/EU version)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game completion state; order restored to New Earth colony

environmental_storytelling.example: Six sectors identified by function: Rocket (Space Development BGM05), Energy (Energy Development BGM06), Bio (Life Research BGM03), Military Base (BASE BGM04), Weather Control (THESKY BGM09), Residential Area (LIFE BGM08), Central Control Center (CENTER BGM02). Each music piece reinforces sector identity and infrastructure purpose.

Alien guard placement and security room design communicate hostile control; database locations require navigation through alien-occupied territory; agent hiding spots suggest resistance/evasion tactics

Power Blade (US/EU) expanded in-game storytelling significantly versus Power Blazer (JP). Agents must be located to grant ID card access, visually communicating that sectors are locked down and requiring authorization.

Power Blade added new title intro story sequence absent from Power Blazer. Story elaboration in-game through cutscenes (stage intro, title demo) establishes narrative context of alien invasion and Nova's mission.

NOVA redesigned from cartoon robot to Arnold Schwarzenegger-like soldier aesthetic in Western localization. Visual shift communicates shift from sci-fi comedic tone to serious military/action narrative; protagonist appearance signals human resistance against alien threat.

Power Blade completely overhauled map screen versus Power Blazer. Hexagonal/circular sector layout with rectangular reticle cursor (vs. player sprite cursor) provides clear hierarchical view of colony structure and stage progression state.

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Boomerang-armed movement through sectors; eight-directional attack capability; health meter decreases on hit
Energy blast attacks in eight directions; increased damage output; visual transformation (helmet/armor equipped)
Sector occupation; attack on player contact; varied sprite designs per sector indicating enemy variety
High-health confrontation in database security rooms; defeat grants sector tape access; visual boss sprite indicates significant threat
Final boss encounter in Control Center; transformation sequence (BGM0C final boss music); defeat triggers colony restoration
Located within sectors via exploration; provides ID card upon contact; communication indicates resistance element
Control program disabled; requires sector tape collection to restore; serves as narrative objective and world-state anchor

notes: Power Blade represents a Mega Man-style level-select platformer with heavy alien invasion narrative. Primary danger communication through enemy presence and environmental hazards (pits); primary safety communication through power-up acquisition and NPC agent contact. Faction conflict centers on human colony (NOVA/Master Computer) versus alien occupation. World-state progression visually marked through sector clearing (data tapes) and map screen updates. US/EU localization (Power Blade) significantly enhanced narrative-through-visuals compared to JP original (Power Blazer) via expanded cutscenes, character redesign, and in-game dialogue. Regional music differences reinforce tonal shift from comedic to serious military action narrative.
